concept.conceptName: pH of Arterial blood adjusted to patient's actual temperature

en.name: pH in arterial blood
en.longDescription: ## Definition & Clinical Context Arterial pH (pHa) is the negative log of the hydrogen ion activity in arterial blood, normally between 7.35 and 7.45. It is the primary descriptor of acid-base balance: pHa < 7.35 defines acidaemia, pHa > 7.45 defines alkalaemia [1]. Together with PaCO2 and bicarbonate, it allows the distinction of respiratory vs metabolic disorders and their compensation. The source data will typically be a single numeric result per patient-timestamp, produced by a blood gas analyser on an arterial sample. pH is dimensionless. **Note: arterial vs venous.** Arterial and venous pH differ by ~ 0.02-0.04 (venous pH is lower because of CO2 production by tissues). The difference widens in low-flow states. Although venous pH is sometimes used as a surrogate for arterial pH in non-critical settings, the two are not strictly interchangeable. This concept set covers **arterial** pH only. Venous, mixed venous, central venous and capillary pH are captured by the separate concept set "pH in venous blood". **Note: temperature-corrected variant.** Modern blood gas analysers measure at 37 °C. A separate LOINC code exists for pH corrected to the patient's actual temperature (relevant in hypothermia, e.g. therapeutic hypothermia post-cardiac arrest). The choice between α-stat (measure at 37 °C) and pH-stat (temperature-corrected) management is a long-standing debate in cardiac and neurosurgery [2]. Both forms are included here; downstream analyses that need to distinguish them must use the LOINC code, not the value. ## Included Concepts The set lists two LOINC "Lab Test" concepts for pH in arterial blood (cord blood excluded): - *3019977 / 2744-1 — pH of Arterial blood (LOINC)*: most widely used. - *3029473 / 33254-4 — pH of Arterial blood adjusted to patient's actual temperature (LOINC)*: temperature-corrected (pH-stat) variant. **Note on units.** pH is dimensionless. LOINC's `EXAMPLE_UCUM_UNITS` is **`[pH]`** (concept 8482 — pH unit). ## Excluded Concepts The following LOINC concepts are deliberately not part of this set: ### Cord blood specimens — physiologically distinct - *3006598 / 28646-8 — pH of Arterial cord blood* - *3030109 / 49698-4 — pH of Arterial cord blood adjusted to patient's actual temperature* ### Other specimens - All venous, mixed venous, central venous, capillary, generic blood codes: captured by the separate "pH in venous blood" concept set or generic-blood concept set if defined. ## Mapping Notes - **Default concept** when the source reports an arterial pH (e.g. blood gas analyser): `3019977 / 2744-1 — pH of Arterial blood`. - **Source reports pH from venous, mixed venous or central venous blood**: do **not** map to this concept set. Use "pH in venous blood" instead. - **Source explicitly says "temperature-corrected" or "pH-stat"**: map to `3029473 / 33254-4`. - **Do not confuse with**: venous pH (separate concept set), urinary pH, gastric pH (different specimens). ## References [1] Regenstrief Institute. LOINC code 2744-1 — pH of Arterial blood. Available: https://loinc.org/2744-1/ [2] Bacher A. Effects of body temperature on blood gases. *Intensive Care Med*. 2005;31(1):24-7. Available: https://doi.org/10.1007/s00134-004-2369-3
